title: Helenium confertiflorum
intro: Helenium confertiflorum, also known as alkali sunflower or prairie sunflower, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its bright yellow daisy-like flowers and slender stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, providing potential health benefits such as wound healing, pain relief, and antimicrobial protection. Bioactive constituents of Helenium confertiflorum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.

This plant is known to grow in a variety of habitats, including dry and rocky areas. The classification of Helenium confertiflorum is further broken down into the genus Helenium and the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of plants. The species Helenium confertiflorum is often found growing among other plants in the same region.
uses: Helenium confertiflorum, also known as common sneezeweed, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and flowers have been used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to their expectorant properties. In addition, the plant's roots have been used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis. Furthermore, Helenium confertiflorum has been used to treat digestive issues, including diarrhea and stomach ulcers.
